/.title: Neural Network
/.content:
Hello, and welcome to my neural network! This is the place where I upload the notes I've taken and compiled from my Mathematics and Computer Science degree at [UQ](https://uq.edu.au/).

The neural network functions very similarly to [Obsidian](https://obsidian.md/);  features like page backlinks, tags, and interactive graphs are supported. You can also full-text search around the neural network with `Ctrl + k`.

/notes/MATH1051/1-eulers-formula.title: Euler's Formula
/notes/MATH1051/1-eulers-formula.content:
*Euler's formula* states that for any real number $x$,
$$e^{ix} = \cos x + i \sin x.$$
When $x = \pi$, Euler's formula can be written in the form $e^{\pi i} + 1 = 0$, which is known as *Euler's identity*.

/notes/MATH1051/2-function.title: Function
/notes/MATH1051/2-function.content:
A *function* $f$ from a set $X$ to a set $Y$, denoted $f: X \to Y$, assigns each element $x \in X$ exactly one element $f(x) \in Y$. The set $X$ is the *domain* of $f$ and the set $Y$ is the *codomain* of $f$.
The *image* or *range* of $f$, that is $f(X)$, is a subset of $Y$. The range is not necessarily equal to the codomain.
